title: hieracium bifidum subsp. wallrothianum
intro: Hieracium bifidum subsp. wallrothianum, also known as Wallroth's hawkweed, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, and is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and acting as an antiseptic agent, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Hieracium bifidum subsp. wallrothianum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
